meta.name: Microsoft 365 Copilot & Agent Administration Fundamentals (AB-900)
meta.description: Foundational concepts for managing Microsoft 365 Copilot and AI agents — deployment, security, governance, compliance, and responsible AI.

cards.definition: An AI-powered assistant integrated into Microsoft 365 apps that uses large language models combined with Microsoft Graph data to generate content, summarize information, and assist with tasks while respecting organizational security boundaries.
cards.hint: AI assistant inside Office apps using your company data.

cards.definition: A unified API that provides access to organizational data such as emails, files, meetings, and relationships. Copilot uses it to ground responses in real business context.
cards.hint: The data source behind Copilot's intelligence.

cards.definition: The process of enriching AI responses with enterprise data from Microsoft Graph to improve accuracy and relevance.
cards.hint: Making AI responses based on real company data.

cards.definition: A deep learning model trained on vast text data to understand and generate human-like language, forming the foundation of Copilot capabilities.
Generates, edits, summarizes, and rewrites documents based on prompts and existing content.
Analyzes data, generates formulas, creates visualizations, and explains insights in natural language.
cards.hint: Ask Excel questions instead of writing formulas.

cards.definition: Creates presentations from prompts or documents, including slides, speaker notes, and design suggestions.
Drafts emails, summarizes threads, and suggests responses to improve communication.
Provides meeting summaries, transcripts, action items, and real-time assistance.
A Copilot experience that works across apps using Microsoft Graph to answer questions and perform tasks across organizational data.
cards.hint: Chat with your entire company's data.

cards.definition: A low-code platform for building and customizing copilots and AI agents with workflows, connectors, and automation.
Autonomous or semi-autonomous systems that perform tasks, automate workflows, and interact with users using AI.
Defined logic and workflows that determine how an AI agent processes inputs and performs actions.
cards.hint: The brain logic of an agent.

cards.definition: Events that start an agent workflow, such as user input or system changes.
Tasks performed by agents, such as sending emails, retrieving data, or updating systems.
Prebuilt integrations that allow Copilot and agents to interact with Microsoft and third-party services.
A secure data platform for storing structured data used by Power Platform and agents.
cards.hint: Backend database for apps and agents.

cards.definition: Designing effective prompts to guide AI outputs toward accurate and useful results.
Predefined instructions that control how an AI model behaves and responds.
Principles ensuring AI is fair, reliable, secure, inclusive, transparent, and accountable.
Ensuring AI systems do not produce biased or discriminatory outcomes.
Providing clarity on how AI systems make decisions.
Ensuring humans remain responsible for AI decisions and outcomes.
Copilot respects existing Microsoft 365 permissions and does not expose unauthorized data.
Policies that prevent sensitive data from being shared outside the organization.
Classifications that protect data with encryption and access controls.
cards.hint: Label data as confidential or public.

cards.definition: A suite of tools for data governance, compliance, and risk management across Microsoft services.
A process for identifying and retrieving electronic data for legal investigations.
Records of user and system activities used for monitoring and compliance.
A security model that requires verification for every access request.
Access control based on user roles and responsibilities.
A security process requiring multiple forms of verification.
A dedicated instance of Microsoft 365 services for an organization.
Copilot requires specific Microsoft 365 licenses and add-ons for access.
cards.hint: You must pay to use Copilot.

cards.definition: Ensuring security, compliance, and data governance are in place before enabling Copilot.
A plan to ensure users effectively use Copilot through training and change management.
cards.hint: Getting users to actually use AI.

cards.definition: Managing organizational changes when introducing AI tools.
Determining where Copilot can provide the most value in business processes.
Efficiency improvements achieved through AI automation and assistance.
A model where humans review and validate AI outputs.
When an AI model generates incorrect or fabricated information.
Mechanisms that prevent harmful or inappropriate AI outputs.
The geographic location where data is stored and processed.
A Microsoft tool that helps track and manage regulatory compliance.
Policies and technologies that safeguard sensitive data.
Organizing data based on sensitivity and importance.
Tools to detect and manage risks from internal users.
Monitoring communications to ensure regulatory compliance.
Rules that determine how long data is stored and when it is deleted.
Evaluating the impact of Copilot using metrics like productivity, efficiency, and user adoption.
